entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in India, particularly in marginal and stress-prone areas.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A major cereal crop in India used for comparison with sorghum.

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A major cereal crop in India used for comparison with sorghum.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study on sorghum cultivars was conducted.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The specific climatic region where sorghum is predominantly grown.

entities.name: MARGINAL AND STRESS-PRONE AREAS
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by low productivity and high stress.

entities.name: NARS
entities.description: National Agricultural Research Systems involved in sorghum crop improvement.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, a stakeholder in sorghum improvement.

entities.name: PRIVATE SEED COMPANIES
entities.description: Commercial entities involved in the development and distribution of sorghum cultivars.

entities.name: IMPROVED CULTIVARS
entities.description: Enhanced varieties of sorghum developed to increase yield and stability.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the sorghum crop.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY LEVELS
entities.description: The mean country-level output of sorghum.

entities.name: YIELD VARIABILITY
entities.description: The fluctuation in sorghum yield across different areas or times.

entities.name: ADOPTION
entities.description: The extent to which farmers use improved sorghum cultivars.

entities.name: DATA ANALYSIS
entities.description: The use of primary and secondary data sources to analyze the impact of improved cultivars.
